approvalTitle: Story Bible — Tasks (agent team breakdown, SB-E/SB-S/integrator)
content:
# Tasks — Story Bible

Execution: one wave, a parallel agent team with disjoint file ownership + an
integrator. Every agent MUST read story-engagement `design.md` §0 (BC1–BC10)
and story-bible `design.md` §0 (SB1–SB6) before its first edit. Requirement
ids (R1–R6) refer to this spec's `requirements.md`; design section refs (§1–§8)
to this spec's `design.md`. Merge gate: full monorepo typecheck + full test
suite green, then dockerized deploy (`docker compose restart convex` /
`restart app`).

Conventions for every task: tolerant-drop at LLM boundaries (BC5), optional
fields + legacy branch (BC9/R16.3), engine purity (BC6), conditional-spread +
`cleanDoc` (SB6), full function paths in scheduler refs (SB3), registry writes
transactional with state writes (SB4).

---

## Agent SB-ENGINE — owns `packages/engine/src/{bible.ts,types.ts,llm.ts}` + engine tests

- [ ] 1.1 SB-E1 — Bible types + `validateProposedBible` + `matchEndingHints` (R1.2, R1.3, R1.5)
  Create `packages/engine/src/bible.ts` with the §1.2 types and the §1.3
  validators: clamp/slug/dedupe per R1.2 bounds, drop lockPlan entries with
  unresolved `keyId` or `gateBand` earlier than the key's `surfaceBand`, null
  when fewer than 4 salvageable keys; `matchEndingHints` reuses
  `normalizeEndingId` semantics (`arc.ts:320`), drops unmatched. Report
  barrel export need to integrator (BC7). Tests: table-driven
  clamp/dedupe/drop matrix, malformed → null, never-throws.
  - _Leverage: `packages/engine/src/arc.ts:70-188` (slugify/clampLen/validateProposedArc pattern), `arc.ts:320` (normalizeEndingId)_
  - _Requirements: R1.2, R1.3, R1.5_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: TypeScript engine developer versed in pure, total functions. Task: create the StoryBible types and validators in packages/engine/src/bible.ts exactly per design §1.2–1.3, mirroring the validateProposedArc clamp discipline. Restrictions: engine stays pure (no console/Date.now — BC6), never throw on malformed input, do not edit the reserved barrel index.ts (report to integrator). Success: vitest table-driven suite green covering every clamp bound, id dedupe, lockPlan drops, under-4-keys → null, endingHints fuzzy-match. Mark the task [-] in tasks.md when starting, log with log-implementation when done, then mark [x]._

- [ ] 1.2 SB-E2 — `itemsEverGranted` ledger (R4.1)
  Optional `itemsEverGranted?: string[]` on `PlayerState` (`types.ts`),
  appended (normalized via `normalizeItemRef`, deduped) in `applyEffect`'s
  `inventory_add` branch (`llm.ts:795`). Read everywhere with `?? []`; NO
  schemaVersion bump/migration (R16.3). Tests: append + dedupe, legacy state
  without the field round-trips untouched.
  - _Leverage: `packages/engine/src/inventory.ts:8-10` (normalizeItemRef), `llm.ts:795-805`_
  - _Requirements: R4.1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: engine developer. Task: add the optional itemsEverGranted ledger per design §1.2, appended on every llm-path inventory_add. Restrictions: field is optional with ?? [] reads — no migration, no schemaVersion change (R16.3); cloneState must carry it. Success: tests prove append/dedupe/normalization and that legacy saves lacking the field behave identically. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 1.3 SB-E3 — `processGatedChoices` registry enforcement + attainability keep-rule (R4.2–R4.5)
  Implement §3 in `bible.ts` (called from `evaluateLlmSceneChoices`,
  `llm.ts:1147-1183`): resolution order inventory/ledger → sibling grant →
  registry (promise) → adopt (caps: 2 promised, 16 registry) → phantom
  unlock via `unlockChoiceResult`. Replace the first-by-array-order keep-rule
  with attainability ordering (promised, then smallest deficit, then array
  order); keep the ≥2-visible rule. Emits `RegistryEvent[]`, never mutates
  the registry input. Empty registry = bible-less behavior (R4.5). Tests:
  the full §8 matrix.
  - _Leverage: `packages/engine/src/llm.ts:1092-1183`, `inventory.ts:19-28` (hasItemTolerant)_
  - _Requirements: R4.2, R4.3, R4.4, R4.5_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: game-systems engine developer. Task: implement processGatedChoices per design §3 and wire it into evaluateLlmSceneChoices. Restrictions: pure function over (results, state, registry, turnNumber); registry input immutable — all changes flow out as RegistryEvents; existing ≥2-visible invariant untouched; sibling-grant check is mandatory (a grant-on-A-gate-B tease must NOT be flagged phantom). Success: table-driven tests cover every §3 branch including cap overflow, bible-less empty registry, and the attainability keep-rule ordering. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 1.4 SB-E4 — Promise seeding + digest builder + refresh merge (R3.1, R3.4, R5.1, R5.2, R6)
  In `bible.ts`: `dueKeySeedings` (promised, ungranted, ≥3 turns — the turn
  loop schedules each via `scheduleThread` with `delayNodes:1`, an
  `inventory_add` effect, and a note derived from label/opensHint);
  `buildBibleDigest` (band filter via `beatBandForTurn`, caps ≤6 keys ≤3
  doors ≤2 twists ≤2 OUTSTANDING KEYS, re-offer line when a promised key is
  granted); `mergeBibleRefresh` (consumed/granted/promised immutable, ≤2 new
  keys, failure → current). Tests: seeding boundaries, digest caps/band
  matrix, merge immutability.
  - _Leverage: `packages/engine/src/delayed.ts:22-37` (scheduleThread), `arc.ts:56-60` (beatBandForTurn)_
  - _Requirements: R3.1, R3.4, R5.1, R5.2, R6_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: engine developer. Task: implement dueKeySeedings, buildBibleDigest, and mergeBibleRefresh per design §1.3, §3, §4. Restrictions: seeding rides the EXISTING state.delayed store via scheduleThread — no parallel store (delayed.ts:16-18 comment); digest is a structured object, no prose rendering in the engine; refresh never removes a key a pending lock references. Success: tests cover seeding turn boundaries, digest band/cap matrix incl. OUTSTANDING KEYS and re-offer states, merge immutability rules. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

## Agent SB-SERVER — owns `convex/llm/storyBible.ts` (new), `convex/llm/{types.ts,responseSchema.ts,prompts/scene.ts}`, `convex/game.ts` touch points, convex tests

- [ ] 2.1 SB-S1 — Response contract: `storyBibleOutputSchema` + wire mirror (R1.2)
  Zod schema in the engine (`bible.ts` or alongside `llmSceneOutputSchema`,
  `llm.ts:327`) shape-checking loosely (tolerant, validated properly by
  1.1); `STORY_BIBLE_RESPONSE_SCHEMA` Gemini wire mirror in
  `convex/llm/responseSchema.ts` (pattern at `:181`); pin both in a
  responseSchema-style test.
  - _Leverage: `packages/engine/src/llm.ts:327`, `convex/llm/responseSchema.ts:181`, existing responseSchema test_
  - _Requirements: R1.2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: LLM-integration developer. Task: define the bible response contract (Zod + Gemini responseSchema mirror) per design §2. Restrictions: schema-level checks stay loose/tolerant (BC5) — hard validation is validateProposedBible's job; keep the two schemas structurally in sync and pinned by a test. Success: pin test green; garbage payload parses to a rejectable shape without throwing. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 2.2 SB-S2 — `convex/llm/storyBible.ts`: prompt, action, persistence, retry (R1.1, R1.4, R2.3)
  Mirror `summarizer.ts` end to end: `buildStoryBiblePrompt` (pure, from the
  same premise/seed fields as the turn-1 request), `generateStoryBible`
  action with the summarizer's provider fan-out (`summarizer.ts:170`),
  parse → `validateProposedBible` → per-string `evaluateTextPolicy`
  (neutralize, R2.3) → `_setStoryBible` internal mutation
  (status ready/failed, retryCount ≤1 reschedule per design §7). Fake-ctx
  tests: success, retry-once, unsalvageable → failed, policy neutralization.
  - _Leverage: `convex/llm/summarizer.ts` (whole-file template), `convex/media/cinematics.ts:590-803` (queue/action split), `convex/contentPolicy.ts` (evaluateTextPolicy)_
  - _Requirements: R1.1, R1.4, R2.3_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Convex backend developer. Task: build the bible generation module per design §2, copying the summarizer skeleton. Restrictions: action never touches the DB directly (SB2); every failure path lands status "failed" silently — a bible failure must never surface to a reader or block a turn (R1.4); scheduler ref strings use the full "llm/storyBible:…" path (SB3). Success: fake-ctx tests cover ready/retry/failed/policy paths; module reported to integrator for the index.ts barrel. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 2.3 SB-S3 — Scheduling from `createSave` + fork copy (R1.1, R1.6, R2.4)
  In the llm-driven fork of `createSave` (`game.ts:408-421`): insert the
  `story_bibles` row (status "queued", `cleanDoc`) + `runAfter(0,
  "llm/storyBible:generateStoryBible", …)`. Fork paths copy the bible row
  as-of the fork point instead of scheduling. No row for authored stories or
  co-op followers. Tests: exactly-one-job matrix.
  - _Leverage: `convex/game.ts:408-466` (llm fork + the deliberate no-media precedent), fork mutation (search `forkPolicy`/fork createSave path)_
  - _Requirements: R1.1, R1.6, R2.4_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: Convex backend developer. Task: schedule the bible job at save creation and copy-on-fork per design §2. Restrictions: llm-driven saves only; never delays the save mutation; swallow scheduling errors like the summarizer call site does (game.ts:2294-2297). Success: fake-ctx tests prove one job per llm save, zero for authored/fork/co-op-follower, fork copies consumption state. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 2.4 SB-S4 — Prompt digest + GATED CHOICE tightening + token budget (R3)
  `SceneGenerationRequest.storyBible?: BibleDigest` in `convex/llm/types.ts`
  (:127 type, :197+ zod); render `storyBibleBlock` per design §4 after
  `storySummaryBlock` (`scene.ts:331`, join at `:420-437`); tighten GATED
  CHOICE (`scene.ts:383`) per R3.3; thread the digest at BOTH assembly sites
  (`game.ts:1582-1585`, `:3733-3736` — SB1) with conditional spread. Extend
  the token-budget snapshot test (`promptScene.test.ts:558`) with the
  worst-case digest fixture, ≤600 tokens over current worst case (R3.4).
  - _Leverage: `convex/llm/prompts/scene.ts:326-437`, `convex/llm/types.ts:127+197`, `convex/tests/promptScene.test.ts:558-591`_
  - _Requirements: R3.1, R3.2, R3.3, R3.4, R3.5_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: prompt-pipeline developer. Task: add the STORY BIBLE digest section and registry-only gating rule per design §4. Restrictions: section renders ONLY when request.storyBible present (bible-less prompts byte-identical to today — R3.5, assert in snapshot); both request-assembly sites updated in lockstep (SB1); budget ≤600 tokens measured by the existing 4-chars heuristic. Success: prompt snapshot + budget tests green with the worst-case fixture. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 2.5 SB-S5 — Turn-mutation integration: attach, registry events, seeding, refresh, analytics (R1.5, R2.1, R4.6, R5, R6)
  In `completeSceneStream` + the non-streaming mirror: first digest
  inclusion patches `attachedAtTurn` + runs `matchEndingHints`; turn
  application calls `processGatedChoices` with the loaded registry and folds
  `RegistryEvent[]` into the bible row IN THE SAME MUTATION as the state
  write (SB4); `dueKeySeedings` → `scheduleThread`; `act_advanced` diff ⇒
  `runAfter` refresh guarded by `lastRefreshAct` → `mergeBibleRefresh`;
  fire the §6 analytics events incl. `choice.locked_shown` once per
  completed turn. Fake-ctx tests per design §8.
  - _Leverage: `convex/game.ts:1614-2300` (completeSceneStream), `:3733+` (mirror), `game.ts:3054-3101` (visibility + locked_denied precedent), `insertStoryAnalytics` (game.ts:3111)_
  - _Requirements: R1.5, R2.1, R4.6, R5.1, R5.2, R6_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: senior Convex developer who owns the turn loop. Task: wire attachment, transactional registry events, key seeding, act-boundary refresh, and analytics per design §2, §3, §6. Restrictions: bible row writes happen in the turn mutation itself, never a follow-up mutation (SB4); locked_shown fires at turn completion, never in the read-path projection (double-count hazard); every bible absence branch is a silent no-op. Success: fake-ctx tests cover attach-once, event folding atomicity, seeding trigger, one-refresh-per-act, analytics payload shapes. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 2.6 SB-S6 — Projection spoiler test (R2.2)
  Assert `projectLlmDrivenScene` output contains no bible-derived keys
  (bible, keyRegistry, lockPlan, twists, endingHints, motifs), matching the
  existing spoiler-test pattern.
  - _Leverage: existing projection spoiler tests (convex/tests, BC10 pattern)_
  - _Requirements: R2.2_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: test engineer. Task: add the BC10 spoiler-absence projection test for every bible field per design §5. Restrictions: test the projection choke point, not component rendering. Success: test fails if any bible key is ever added to the projection, passes today. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

## INTEGRATOR — owns reserved files (`convex/schema.ts`, `convex/index.ts`, `convex/crons.ts`)

- [ ] 3.1 SB-I1 — Schema + barrel (R2.1, BC7)
  Add the `story_bibles` table per design §1.1 (`by_saveId` index) and the
  `llm/storyBible` barrel export. Land agents' reported needs; keep edits
  additive. Then: full monorepo typecheck + all suites + dockerized deploy
  (`docker compose restart convex` / `restart app`) as the merge gate.
  - _Leverage: `convex/schema.ts:431` (daily_tales template), `convex/index.ts`_
  - _Requirements: R2.1_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: integrator with sole ownership of reserved files. Task: land the story_bibles table and barrel export per design §1.1, integrate all agent branches. Restrictions: additive edits only to schema.ts/index.ts/crons.ts; nothing merges until typecheck + full suites are green. Success: dockerized deploy healthy; bible.generated events visible for a fresh llm save on cyoa-local. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._

- [ ] 3.2 SB-I2 — Live verification + telemetry baseline (R4.6)
  On cyoa-local: create a fresh llm save, verify the bible row reaches
  "ready" during turn 1, play ≥6 turns, confirm digest in prompt logs, at
  least one promised→seeded key cycle end to end, and `choice.locked_shown`
  / `bible.*` events landing in `analytics_events`. Record the pre-launch
  phantom-gate rate from locked_shown rows where the item was neither
  ever-granted nor in the registry, as the success-metric baseline.
  - _Leverage: BC8 deploy flow, operator dashboard / analytics_events queries_
  - _Requirements: R4.6, NFR Reliability_
  - _Prompt: Implement the task for spec story-bible, first run spec-workflow-guide to get the workflow guide then implement the task: Role: release verifier. Task: drive the full loop on the dockerized cyoa-local env and capture the telemetry baseline. Restrictions: verify through real play (streamed turns), not unit tests; do not ship if a bible failure surfaces to the reader in any form. Success: documented run with event samples; baseline phantom-gate rate recorded in the implementation log. Mark [-] when starting, log-implementation when done, then [x]._
